delegationPacket.objective: Clarify the operator need, scope boundaries, constraints, and success signal for the feature before design or implementation starts.
delegationPacket.inputContext:
Feature Name

In-App Context-Aware Assistant for Structured System Actions

Summary

Implement an always-available assistant inside the agent management system that allows the user to interact with the system using natural language and, eventually, voice input. The assistant should understand requests like creating goals, tasks, roles, agents, workflows, or other records, ask for clarification when needed, and convert user intent into structured actions within the system.

The assistant should also be context-aware, meaning it should have access to relevant information about what the user is currently viewing or interacting with in the application. This should help it interpret instructions more accurately, infer relationships more intelligently, reduce unnecessary clarification questions, and perform more relevant actions.

The purpose of this feature is to reduce friction when using the agent management system by allowing the user to simply tell the system what they want created or changed instead of manually navigating forms and screens for every action.

⸻

Problem

The current system likely requires too much manual interaction to create or manage structured records such as tasks, goals, roles, and instructions. This adds friction, especially when the user already knows what they want and could describe it much faster through speech or text.

A generic assistant without awareness of the current application state would still force the user to over-explain things that the system should already know. If the user is on a specific project page, workflow page, agent detail page, role detail page, or task list, the assistant should use that context to better understand what “this,” “here,” “under this,” “related to this,” or “add one for this” refers to.

The system therefore needs a faster, more natural interface layer that sits on top of the structured data model and current UI context and can interpret user intent accordingly.

Examples of friction this should solve:

* User has to manually open forms and fill fields one by one
* User has to remember where in the system a record type lives
* User has to translate thoughts into the system’s structure manually
* User has to repeat context the app already knows
* Voice-based idea capture and action entry are not smoothly supported
* The assistant may misinterpret intent without awareness of the current page or selected object

⸻

Desired Outcome

The user should be able to open an assistant panel and say or type things like:

* “Create a goal for launching the onboarding prototype.”
* “Make a task called ‘Create web developer role.’”
* “Create a role called Web Developer with instructions saying it is an expert in web development, frontend architecture, responsive UI, and debugging.”
* “Add a task under this project for redesigning the workflow page.”
* “Create an agent based on this role.”
* “Make this into a feature request.”
* “Add a child task for this.”
* “What related tasks already exist here?”

The assistant should:

1. Understand the request
2. Use current UI/page context to interpret references and intent
3. Infer the intended object type when possible
4. Extract structured fields
5. Ask follow-up questions only when required
6. Show the user a preview of what it plans to create or do
7. Let the user confirm, edit, or cancel
8. Create the record(s) in the system

⸻

Primary Goal

Create a usable, context-aware assistant interface that can translate conversational input into structured system actions for the agent management system.

⸻

Scope for V1

V1 should focus on a narrow but useful version of the feature.

V1 should support:

* Text input to the assistant
* Optional paste-in transcript input
* Interpreting requests to create:
    * Tasks
    * Goals
    * Roles
    * Agents
* Structured field extraction from natural language
* Awareness of current page, selected object, and relevant local context
* Preview before save
* Confirmation before record creation
* Basic clarification flow when required
* Error handling when intent is unclear
* Logging of assistant actions, context used, and created records

V1 should not try to fully solve:

* Complex multi-step workflow orchestration
* Fully autonomous execution of broad system changes
* Advanced voice/audio recording pipeline if that adds too much complexity
* Deep cross-object reasoning beyond straightforward create/update flows
* Open-ended destructive actions without confirmation

⸻

Context Awareness Requirement

Definition

The assistant should be aware of relevant application context at the time the user submits a request. This means it should receive structured information about what the user is currently viewing or interacting with so that it can interpret the request more accurately.

This context may include:

* current page or route
* current object type
* current object ID
* currently viewed record details
* selected item(s)
* parent object or breadcrumb context
* surrounding list/filter/search state if relevant
* current project/workflow/role/agent/task the user is inside
* available actions valid for the current context

Examples

If the user is on:

* a project page, “create a task for this” should default to creating a task linked to that project
* a role detail page, “create an agent for this” should infer that “this” refers to the current role
* a workflow page, “add a step about validation” should infer the workflow as parent context
* a task detail page, “make this blocked” or “add a child task” should use that task as the target
* an agent list page, “create one for documentation” should infer that the user likely means an agent, not some unrelated object type

The assistant should reduce ambiguity by using available context, but still show a preview before committing actions.

⸻

Functional Requirements

1. Assistant Entry Point

Implement an assistant that is easy to access from anywhere in the agent management system.

Possible UX patterns:

* Persistent floating assistant button
* Slide-over panel
* Global command bar with assistant mode
* Dedicated assistant page with chat history

Minimum requirement:

* Assistant can be opened from the main application UI without deep navigation

⸻

2. Input Modes

V1

* Text input
* Support for pasted voice transcripts

V2+

* Direct voice recording
* Speech-to-text transcription
* Optional playback and transcript editing before submit

⸻

3. Context Injection

When the assistant is opened or when the user submits a request, the system should provide the assistant with structured context about the current UI state.

This context should be passed in a controlled, structured form rather than forcing the assistant to infer everything from raw rendered text.

Example context payload:

{
  "route": "/projects/123/tasks",
  "pageType": "project_detail",
  "currentObject": {
    "type": "project",
    "id": "123",
    "name": "Onboarding Prototype"
  },
  "selectedObjects": [],
  "breadcrumbs": [
    { "type": "workspace", "id": "w1", "name": "AdventureWare" },
    { "type": "project", "id": "123", "name": "Onboarding Prototype" }
  ],
  "visibleCapabilities": [
    "create_task",
    "create_goal",
    "view_project",
    "link_existing_task"
  ]
}

The assistant should use this context to improve intent recognition and payload generation.

⸻

4. Intent Recognition

The assistant should classify what the user is trying to do.

Initial supported intents:

* Create task
* Create goal
* Create role
* Create agent
* Ask system question
* Request clarification/help

Intent recognition should consider:

* the user’s raw input
* the current page context
* the currently viewed object
* the kinds of actions that are valid in the current location

The system should infer likely intent from phrasing and context, but not rely on exact keywords.

⸻

5. Structured Field Extraction

The assistant should extract relevant fields from the user’s natural-language input.

Example mappings

Task

* name/title
* description/instructions
* parent object/project if mentioned or inferred from context
* priority if mentioned
* tags if useful
* status default

Goal

* name/title
* description
* timeframe if mentioned
* parent area/project if mentioned or inferred from context

Role

* name/title
* description
* instructions
* specialty/focus areas

Agent

* name/title
* linked role if mentioned or inferred from context
* instructions
* capabilities or tools if supported
* purpose

The assistant should populate as much as possible automatically, including context-derived defaults, then present a draft.

⸻

6. Clarification Flow

If the user gives incomplete or ambiguous instructions, the assistant should ask focused follow-up questions.

Examples:

* “Do you want this created as a task or a goal?”
* “Should this role also include backend development, or just frontend/web?”
* “Should I attach this to the current project?”
* “Do you want me to create the role only, or also create an agent that uses it?”

Clarification should be minimal and only used when required after considering available context.

⸻

7. Preview Before Commit

Before saving, the assistant should show a structured preview of what it plans to create.

Example:

Create Role

* Name: Web Developer
* Description: Expert in web development
* Instructions: Specializes in frontend architecture, responsive UI, debugging, and web app implementation
* Context Used: Current page = Roles, Parent workspace = AdventureWare

Actions:

* Confirm
* Edit
* Cancel

This is important because the assistant may interpret things imperfectly and should not silently create bad records.

⸻

8. Record Creation

After confirmation, the assistant should create the record in the system using the same underlying creation logic used by standard forms or APIs.

Important:

* Assistant-created records should use the same validation rules as manually created records
* No special inconsistent backend path
* Reuse domain services where possible
* Context-derived relationships should still be validated before commit

⸻

9. Action Log / Traceability

Every assistant action should be logged.

Track:

* raw user input
* interpreted intent
* extracted fields
* context snapshot used for interpretation
* follow-up questions asked
* final confirmed payload
* result of creation
* errors if any

This will help debugging, trust, refinement, and prompt iteration.

⸻

10. Safety / Guardrails

The assistant should not perform destructive or high-risk actions without clear confirmation.

For V1:

* Focus primarily on create flows
* If update/delete is added later, always require explicit confirmation
* If the assistant is uncertain, it should say so instead of guessing aggressively
* Context should improve confidence, not justify unsafe silent actions

⸻

UX Requirements

Core UX Principles

* Fast
* Low friction
* Clear
* Reversible
* Trustworthy
* Structured without feeling rigid
* Context-aware without feeling opaque

Desired Feel

The assistant should feel like a practical operator layer for the system, not like a generic chatbot. It should be specifically good at understanding the objects, workflows, and current application context of the agent management system.

UX Flow Example

1. User opens assistant
2. User types or pastes transcript
3. Assistant receives current page/context metadata
4. Assistant interprets request using both user input and app context
5. Assistant asks follow-up only if necessary
6. Assistant shows a structured draft
7. User confirms
8. Assistant creates record
9. Assistant returns success message and link to the new record

⸻

Example User Stories

Story 1

As a user, I want to tell the assistant to create a task in plain English so I do not have to manually fill out a task form.

Story 2

As a user, I want the assistant to understand what page or record I am currently viewing so I do not have to restate obvious context.

Story 3

As a user, I want to dictate a role description by voice transcript so I can capture ideas quickly when thinking out loud.

Story 4

As a user, I want the assistant to convert my messy phrasing into structured fields so the system remains organized without slowing me down.

Story 5

As a user, I want to review and confirm what the assistant plans to create before it saves anything.

Story 6

As a user, I want the assistant to understand the system’s objects and my current location well enough that it feels like a specialized operator, not a generic chat box.

⸻

Example Inputs and Expected Behavior

Example 1

Context: User is on project “Onboarding Prototype”
Input:
“Create a task called Create Web Developer Role. Instructions are to create a web developer role that is an expert at web development.”

Expected behavior:
Assistant identifies intent = create task
Extracts:

* name = Create Web Developer Role
* instructions = Create a web developer role that is an expert at web development
* parent project = Onboarding Prototype (from context)

Shows preview, then creates task on confirmation.

⸻

Example 2

Context: User is on role detail page for “Frontend Engineer”
Input:
“Create an agent for this.”

Expected behavior:
Assistant identifies intent = create agent
Infers linked role = Frontend Engineer from context
Shows preview
Creates agent on confirmation

⸻

Example 3

Context: User is on workflow page
Input:
“I need something to track improving this page.”

Expected behavior:
Assistant uses current page context to infer likely relation to workflow or feature work
Still detects ambiguity between task/goal/feature request
Asks:
“Do you want this created as a task, a goal, or a feature request?”

⸻

Technical Implementation Requirements

Architecture Guidance

Build this as a thin assistant layer on top of the existing domain model and create/update services.

Do not hardcode brittle UI-only logic if the same operations already exist in backend/domain services.

Recommended architecture:

1. Assistant UI
2. Context provider / context serializer
3. Assistant orchestration layer
4. Intent parsing / field extraction layer
5. Domain action layer
6. Logging/audit layer

⸻

Suggested System Components

1. Assistant UI Component

Responsible for:

* rendering conversation/messages
* input box
* submission
* preview cards
* clarification prompts
* confirm/edit/cancel actions

2. Context Provider

Responsible for:

* reading current route and UI state
* identifying current object and parent object
* gathering selected/active entities
* exposing context in a clean structured format
* limiting context to useful, relevant information

3. Intent + Extraction Layer

Responsible for:

* interpreting raw natural language
* classifying object type and action type
* using context to improve interpretation
* extracting structured fields
* returning confidence + missing fields

This can initially be rule-assisted and/or LLM-assisted depending on the prototype approach.

4. Action Planner

Responsible for converting interpreted intent plus context into a system action plan like:

{
  "action": "create_task",
  "payload": {
    "name": "Create Web Developer Role",
    "instructions": "Create a web developer role that is an expert at web development.",
    "projectId": "123"
  },
  "contextUsed": {
    "pageType": "project_detail",
    "currentObject": {
      "type": "project",
      "id": "123",
      "name": "Onboarding Prototype"
    }
  },
  "missingFields": [],
  "needsConfirmation": true
}

5. Domain Executor

Responsible for:

* validating payload
* calling actual create services
* returning created object metadata

6. Audit Log

Responsible for:

* storing transcript/input
* context snapshot
* interpreted action
* payload
* outcome

⸻

Recommended V1 Design Strategy

Keep V1 narrow and reliable

Do not try to make it an all-knowing general assistant yet.

Instead, make it very good at:

* understanding a handful of object types
* creating records reliably
* using current app context intelligently
* showing previews
* asking minimal clarifying questions

Prefer a structured intermediate representation

Do not let the assistant directly mutate the database from freeform chat output.

Always convert assistant output into a structured action plan first.

Reuse existing create logic

If tasks, goals, roles, and agents already have forms/services, the assistant should call into those instead of duplicating business logic.

Make context explicit

Do not rely on vague hidden magic. Keep context gathering explicit, inspectable, and debuggable.

Make it debug-friendly

You will want to inspect:

* what the user said
* what context the assistant received
* what the assistant thought they meant
* why it asked a question
* what payload it produced

⸻

Acceptance Criteria

Minimum acceptance criteria for V1

* User can open assistant from the app UI
* User can enter natural language text
* Assistant receives current page/context metadata
* Assistant can interpret requests to create tasks, goals, roles, or agents
* Assistant can use current context to improve interpretation and linking
* Assistant can extract key fields from input
* Assistant asks for clarification when intent is insufficiently clear
* Assistant shows a preview before creation
* User can confirm or cancel
* Record is created successfully in the system after confirmation
* Assistant-generated actions are logged, including context used
* Error states are handled cleanly

⸻

Non-Functional Requirements

Performance

* Assistant interaction should feel responsive
* Preview generation should happen quickly enough to feel conversational

Reliability

* Assistant should fail safely
* Ambiguous cases should not silently create incorrect records

Maintainability

* Assistant action types should be modular and extensible
* New object types and new context providers should be easy to add later

Usability

* The feature should reduce friction, not create more
* The assistant must be simpler than manual form entry for common cases

⸻

Nice-to-Have Features After V1

* Voice recording button with transcription
* Context-aware suggestions based on current page
* “Create from this page”
* “Turn this into a task”
* Edit assistant-created payload before save
* Suggested field autofill
* Multi-record creation from one input
* Search existing records before creating duplicates
* Links to created objects
* In-session contextual memory
* Deeper page-aware assistant actions beyond create flows

⸻

Risks / Challenges

* Overly broad assistant scope could make implementation messy
* Misclassification of user intent could create bad data
* Poor context modeling could cause confusing behavior
* Trying to support too many object types too early could slow progress
* Voice support can become a distraction if included too early
* Trust will drop quickly if the assistant feels generic or inaccurate

⸻

Recommended Phased Implementation

Phase 1

Implement text-only assistant for create flows:

* create task
* create goal
* create role
* create agent

Include:

* current page awareness
* current object awareness
* structured preview and confirmation

Phase 2

Improve disambiguation and context awareness:

* infer parent project/context more reliably
* suggest links to related records
* reduce follow-up questions
* support selected object context

Phase 3

Add voice input:

* record audio
* transcribe
* edit transcript before submit

Phase 4

Support update/search/query flows:

* “show me tasks related to this”
* “update this role”
* “mark this task as blocked”

⸻

Deliverables

The implementation should result in:

1. An assistant UI in the agent management system
2. Natural-language-to-structured-action parsing for V1 object types
3. Context injection from current UI/page state
4. Preview and confirmation flow
5. Record creation integration
6. Action logging
7. Basic documentation for how the assistant works and how to extend it

⸻

The user should be able to open an assistant panel and say or type things like:

* “Create a goal for launching the onboarding prototype.”
* “Make a task called ‘Create web developer role.’”
* “Create a role called Web Developer with instructions saying it is an expert in web development, frontend architecture, responsive UI, and debugging.”
* “Add a task under this project for redesigning the workflow page.”
* “Create an agent based on this role.”
* “Make this into a feature request.”
* “Add a child task for this.”
* “What related tasks already exist here?”

The assistant should:

1. Understand the request
2. Use current UI/page context to interpret references and intent
3. Infer the intended object type when possible
4. Extract structured fields
5. Ask follow-up questions only when required
6. Show the user a preview of what it plans to create or do
7. Let the user confirm, edit, or cancel
8. Create the record(s) in the system

⸻

Primary Goal

Create a usable, context-aware assistant interface that can translate conversational input into structured system actions for the agent management system.

⸻

Scope for V1

V1 should focus on a narrow but useful version of the feature.

V1 should support:

* Text input to the assistant
* Optional paste-in transcript input
* Interpreting requests to create:
    * Tasks
    * Goals
    * Roles
    * Agents
* Structured field extraction from natural language
* Awareness of current page, selected object, and relevant local context
* Preview before save
* Confirmation before record creation
* Basic clarification flow when required
* Error handling when intent is unclear
* Logging of assistant actions, context used, and created records

V1 should not try to fully solve:

* Complex multi-step workflow orchestration
* Fully autonomous execution of broad system changes
* Advanced voice/audio recording pipeline if that adds too much complexity
* Deep cross-object reasoning beyond straightforward create/update flows
* Open-ended destructive actions without confirmation

⸻

Context Awareness Requirement

Definition

The assistant should be aware of relevant application context at the time the user submits a request. This means it should receive structured information about what the user is currently viewing or interacting with so that it can interpret the request more accurately.

This context may include:

* current page or route
* current object type
* current object ID
* currently viewed record details
* selected item(s)
* parent object or breadcrumb context
* surrounding list/filter/search state if relevant
* current project/workflow/role/agent/task the user is inside
* available actions valid for the current context

Examples

If the user is on:

* a project page, “create a task for this” should default to creating a task linked to that project
* a role detail page, “create an agent for this” should infer that “this” refers to the current role
* a workflow page, “add a step about validation” should infer the workflow as parent context
* a task detail page, “make this blocked” or “add a child task” should use that task as the target
* an agent list page, “create one for documentation” should infer that the user likely means an agent, not some unrelated object type

The assistant should reduce ambiguity by using available context, but still show a preview before committing actions.

⸻

Functional Requirements

1. Assistant Entry Point

Implement an assistant that is easy to access from anywhere in the agent management system.

Possible UX patterns:

* Persistent floating assistant button
* Slide-over panel
* Global command bar with assistant mode
* Dedicated assistant page with chat history

Minimum requirement:

* Assistant can be opened from the main application UI without deep navigation

⸻

2. Input Modes

V1

* Text input
* Support for pasted voice transcripts

V2+

* Direct voice recording
* Speech-to-text transcription
* Optional playback and transcript editing before submit

⸻

3. Context Injection

When the assistant is opened or when the user submits a request, the system should provide the assistant with structured context about the current UI state.

This context should be passed in a controlled, structured form rather than forcing the assistant to infer everything from raw rendered text.

Example context payload:

{
  "route": "/projects/123/tasks",
  "pageType": "project_detail",
  "currentObject": {
    "type": "project",
    "id": "123",
    "name": "Onboarding Prototype"
  },
  "selectedObjects": [],
  "breadcrumbs": [
    { "type": "workspace", "id": "w1", "name": "AdventureWare" },
    { "type": "project", "id": "123", "name": "Onboarding Prototype" }
  ],
  "visibleCapabilities": [
    "create_task",
    "create_goal",
    "view_project",
    "link_existing_task"
  ]
}

The assistant should use this context to improve intent recognition and payload generation.

⸻

4. Intent Recognition

The assistant should classify what the user is trying to do.

Initial supported intents:

* Create task
* Create goal
* Create role
* Create agent
* Ask system question
* Request clarification/help

Intent recognition should consider:

* the user’s raw input
* the current page context
* the currently viewed object
* the kinds of actions that are valid in the current location

The system should infer likely intent from phrasing and context, but not rely on exact keywords.

⸻

5. Structured Field Extraction

The assistant should extract relevant fields from the user’s natural-language input.

Example mappings

Task

* name/title
* description/instructions
* parent object/project if mentioned or inferred from context
* priority if mentioned
* tags if useful
* status default

Goal

* name/title
* description
* timeframe if mentioned
* parent area/project if mentioned or inferred from context

Role

* name/title
* description
* instructions
* specialty/focus areas

Agent

* name/title
* linked role if mentioned or inferred from context
* instructions
* capabilities or tools if supported
* purpose

The assistant should populate as much as possible automatically, including context-derived defaults, then present a draft.

⸻

6. Clarification Flow

If the user gives incomplete or ambiguous instructions, the assistant should ask focused follow-up questions.

Examples:

* “Do you want this created as a task or a goal?”
* “Should this role also include backend development, or just frontend/web?”
* “Should I attach this to the current project?”
* “Do you want me to create the role only, or also create an agent that uses it?”

Clarification should be minimal and only used when required after considering available context.

⸻

7. Preview Before Commit

Before saving, the assistant should show a structured preview of what it plans to create.

Example:

Create Role

* Name: Web Developer
* Description: Expert in web development
* Instructions: Specializes in frontend architecture, responsive UI, debugging, and web app implementation
* Context Used: Current page = Roles, Parent workspace = AdventureWare

Actions:

* Confirm
* Edit
* Cancel

This is important because the assistant may interpret things imperfectly and should not silently create bad records.

⸻

8. Record Creation

After confirmation, the assistant should create the record in the system using the same underlying creation logic used by standard forms or APIs.

Important:

* Assistant-created records should use the same validation rules as manually created records
* No special inconsistent backend path
* Reuse domain services where possible
* Context-derived relationships should still be validated before commit

⸻

9. Action Log / Traceability

Every assistant action should be logged.

Track:

* raw user input
* interpreted intent
* extracted fields
* context snapshot used for interpretation
* follow-up questions asked
* final confirmed payload
* result of creation
* errors if any

This will help debugging, trust, refinement, and prompt iteration.

⸻

10. Safety / Guardrails

The assistant should not perform destructive or high-risk actions without clear confirmation.

For V1:

* Focus primarily on create flows
* If update/delete is added later, always require explicit confirmation
* If the assistant is uncertain, it should say so instead of guessing aggressively
* Context should improve confidence, not justify unsafe silent actions

⸻

UX Requirements

Core UX Principles

* Fast
* Low friction
* Clear
* Reversible
* Trustworthy
* Structured without feeling rigid
* Context-aware without feeling opaque

Desired Feel

The assistant should feel like a practical operator layer for the system, not like a generic chatbot. It should be specifically good at understanding the objects, workflows, and current application context of the agent management system.

UX Flow Example

1. User opens assistant
2. User types or pastes transcript
3. Assistant receives current page/context metadata
4. Assistant interprets request using both user input and app context
5. Assistant asks follow-up only if necessary
6. Assistant shows a structured draft
7. User confirms
8. Assistant creates record
9. Assistant returns success message and link to the new record

⸻

Example User Stories

Story 1

As a user, I want to tell the assistant to create a task in plain English so I do not have to manually fill out a task form.

Story 2

As a user, I want the assistant to understand what page or record I am currently viewing so I do not have to restate obvious context.

Story 3

As a user, I want to dictate a role description by voice transcript so I can capture ideas quickly when thinking out loud.

Story 4

As a user, I want the assistant to convert my messy phrasing into structured fields so the system remains organized without slowing me down.

Story 5

As a user, I want to review and confirm what the assistant plans to create before it saves anything.

Story 6

As a user, I want the assistant to understand the system’s objects and my current location well enough that it feels like a specialized operator, not a generic chat box.

⸻

Example Inputs and Expected Behavior

Example 1

Context: User is on project “Onboarding Prototype”
Input:
“Create a task called Create Web Developer Role. Instructions are to create a web developer role that is an expert at web development.”

Expected behavior:
Assistant identifies intent = create task
Extracts:

* name = Create Web Developer Role
* instructions = Create a web developer role that is an expert at web development
* parent project = Onboarding Prototype (from context)

Shows preview, then creates task on confirmation.

⸻

Example 2

Context: User is on role detail page for “Frontend Engineer”
Input:
“Create an agent for this.”

Expected behavior:
Assistant identifies intent = create agent
Infers linked role = Frontend Engineer from context
Shows preview
Creates agent on confirmation

⸻

Example 3

Context: User is on workflow page
Input:
“I need something to track improving this page.”

Expected behavior:
Assistant uses current page context to infer likely relation to workflow or feature work
Still detects ambiguity between task/goal/feature request
Asks:
“Do you want this created as a task, a goal, or a feature request?”

⸻

Technical Implementation Requirements

Architecture Guidance

Build this as a thin assistant layer on top of the existing domain model and create/update services.

Do not hardcode brittle UI-only logic if the same operations already exist in backend/domain services.

Recommended architecture:

1. Assistant UI
2. Context provider / context serializer
3. Assistant orchestration layer
4. Intent parsing / field extraction layer
5. Domain action layer
6. Logging/audit layer

⸻

Suggested System Components

1. Assistant UI Component

Responsible for:

* rendering conversation/messages
* input box
* submission
* preview cards
* clarification prompts
* confirm/edit/cancel actions

2. Context Provider

Responsible for:

* reading current route and UI state
* identifying current object and parent object
* gathering selected/active entities
* exposing context in a clean structured format
* limiting context to useful, relevant information

3. Intent + Extraction Layer

Responsible for:

* interpreting raw natural language
* classifying object type and action type
* using context to improve interpretation
* extracting structured fields
* returning confidence + missing fields

This can initially be rule-assisted and/or LLM-assisted depending on the prototype approach.

4. Action Planner

Responsible for converting interpreted intent plus context into a system action plan like:

{
  "action": "create_task",
  "payload": {
    "name": "Create Web Developer Role",
    "instructions": "Create a web developer role that is an expert at web development.",
    "projectId": "123"
  },
  "contextUsed": {
    "pageType": "project_detail",
    "currentObject": {
      "type": "project",
      "id": "123",
      "name": "Onboarding Prototype"
    }
  },
  "missingFields": [],
  "needsConfirmation": true
}

5. Domain Executor

Responsible for:

* validating payload
* calling actual create services
* returning created object metadata

6. Audit Log

Responsible for:

* storing transcript/input
* context snapshot
* interpreted action
* payload
* outcome

⸻

Recommended V1 Design Strategy

Keep V1 narrow and reliable

Do not try to make it an all-knowing general assistant yet.

Instead, make it very good at:

* understanding a handful of object types
* creating records reliably
* using current app context intelligently
* showing previews
* asking minimal clarifying questions

Prefer a structured intermediate representation

Do not let the assistant directly mutate the database from freeform chat output.

Always convert assistant output into a structured action plan first.

Reuse existing create logic

If tasks, goals, roles, and agents already have forms/services, the assistant should call into those instead of duplicating business logic.

Make context explicit

Do not rely on vague hidden magic. Keep context gathering explicit, inspectable, and debuggable.

Make it debug-friendly

You will want to inspect:

* what the user said
* what context the assistant received
* what the assistant thought they meant
* why it asked a question
* what payload it produced

⸻

Acceptance Criteria

Minimum acceptance criteria for V1

* User can open assistant from the app UI
* User can enter natural language text
* Assistant receives current page/context metadata
* Assistant can interpret requests to create tasks, goals, roles, or agents
* Assistant can use current context to improve interpretation and linking
* Assistant can extract key fields from input
* Assistant asks for clarification when intent is insufficiently clear
* Assistant shows a preview before creation
* User can confirm or cancel
* Record is created successfully in the system after confirmation
* Assistant-generated actions are logged, including context used
* Error states are handled cleanly

⸻

Non-Functional Requirements

Performance

* Assistant interaction should feel responsive
* Preview generation should happen quickly enough to feel conversational

Reliability

* Assistant should fail safely
* Ambiguous cases should not silently create incorrect records

Maintainability

* Assistant action types should be modular and extensible
* New object types and new context providers should be easy to add later

Usability

* The feature should reduce friction, not create more
* The assistant must be simpler than manual form entry for common cases

⸻

Nice-to-Have Features After V1

* Voice recording button with transcription
* Context-aware suggestions based on current page
* “Create from this page”
* “Turn this into a task”
* Edit assistant-created payload before save
* Suggested field autofill
* Multi-record creation from one input
* Search existing records before creating duplicates
* Links to created objects
* In-session contextual memory
* Deeper page-aware assistant actions beyond create flows

⸻

Risks / Challenges

* Overly broad assistant scope could make implementation messy
* Misclassification of user intent could create bad data
* Poor context modeling could cause confusing behavior
* Trying to support too many object types too early could slow progress
* Voice support can become a distraction if included too early
* Trust will drop quickly if the assistant feels generic or inaccurate

⸻

Recommended Phased Implementation

Phase 1

Implement text-only assistant for create flows:

* create task
* create goal
* create role
* create agent

Include:

* current page awareness
* current object awareness
* structured preview and confirmation

Phase 2

Improve disambiguation and context awareness:

* infer parent project/context more reliably
* suggest links to related records
* reduce follow-up questions
* support selected object context

Phase 3

Add voice input:

* record audio
* transcribe
* edit transcript before submit

Phase 4

Support update/search/query flows:

* “show me tasks related to this”
* “update this role”
* “mark this task as blocked”

⸻

Deliverables

The implementation should result in:

1. An assistant UI in the agent management system
2. Natural-language-to-structured-action parsing for V1 object types
3. Context injection from current UI/page state
4. Preview and confirmation flow
5. Record creation integration
6. Action logging
7. Basic documentation for how the assistant works and how to extend it

⸻

Updated Instruction Block for Codex

Implement a V1 in-app context-aware assistant for the agent management system.
Goal:
Create an always-available assistant UI that allows the user to type natural-language instructions and have the system interpret them into structured actions for creating records such as tasks, goals, roles, and agents. The assistant must also use current application context so it can better understand what the user is looking at and interpret instructions more accurately.
Requirements:
- Add an assistant entry point accessible from the main app UI
- Support text input in V1
- Allow users to enter instructions like:
  - “Create a task called Create Web Developer Role”
  - “Create a role named Web Developer that is an expert in frontend development”
  - “Create a goal for improving onboarding”
  - “Create an agent for this”
  - “Add a task under this project”
- Build a context provider that passes structured current-page/app-state context into the assistant, including where possible:
  - current route
  - page type
  - current object type/id/name
  - selected objects
  - parent/breadcrumb context
  - valid actions for the current screen
- Build an interpretation layer that:
  - identifies user intent
  - determines the target object type
  - uses current context to improve interpretation
  - extracts structured fields from freeform input
  - identifies missing required fields
- Before creating anything, show a preview of the interpreted result and require user confirmation
- If intent or required fields are ambiguous, ask a focused clarification question
- After confirmation, create the record using the system’s normal domain/service layer rather than duplicating creation logic
- Log the raw input, context snapshot, interpreted action, extracted payload, and final result for debugging/audit purposes
V1 scope:
- Create flows only
- Supported object types: task, goal, role, agent
- No destructive actions
- No full autonomous multi-step workflows yet
- Voice can be deferred, but structure the code so voice transcript input can be added later
Implementation guidance:
- Keep the assistant architecture modular
- Add a dedicated context provider / serializer layer
- Use a structured intermediate action plan before executing any operation
- Reuse existing validation and creation services
- Optimize for reliability and clarity over cleverness
- Make context usage explicit and debuggable
- Make it easy to extend with more action types later
Deliverables:
- Assistant UI
- Context provider
- Intent/extraction layer
- Preview and confirmation flow
- Record creation integration
- Logging/audit trail
- Clear code organization and brief implementation notes

⸻

Shorter Updated Task Version

Title:
Implement context-aware in-app assistant for agent management system

Description:
Add an always-available assistant to the agent management system that lets the user create structured records such as tasks, goals, roles, and agents using natural language. The assistant should use both the user’s input and the current application context, such as the page being viewed and the current object, to interpret requests more accurately. It should extract relevant fields, ask clarification questions when needed, show a preview before saving, and create records through the normal system logic. Design V1 around text input first, but keep the architecture ready for future voice/transcript input.

Acceptance Criteria:

* Assistant accessible from main UI
* Supports text-based natural language input
* Receives current page/object context
* Can create tasks, goals, roles, and agents
* Uses context to improve interpretation and linking
* Extracts fields from freeform input
* Asks clarifying questions when needed
* Shows preview before create
* Requires confirmation
* Uses existing creation logic
* Logs assistant actions, including context used

delegationPacket.inputContext:
Feature Name

In-App Context-Aware Assistant for Structured System Actions

Summary

Implement an always-available assistant inside the agent management system that allows the user to interact with the system using natural language and, eventually, voice input. The assistant should understand requests like creating goals, tasks, roles, agents, workflows, or other records, ask for clarification when needed, and convert user intent into structured actions within the system.

The assistant should also be context-aware, meaning it should have access to relevant information about what the user is currently viewing or interacting with in the application. This should help it interpret instructions more accurately, infer relationships more intelligently, reduce unnecessary clarification questions, and perform more relevant actions.

The purpose of this feature is to reduce friction when using the agent management system by allowing the user to simply tell the system what they want created or changed instead of manually navigating forms and screens for every action.

⸻

Problem

The current system likely requires too much manual interaction to create or manage structured records such as tasks, goals, roles, and instructions. This adds friction, especially when the user already knows what they want and could describe it much faster through speech or text.

A generic assistant without awareness of the current application state would still force the user to over-explain things that the system should already know. If the user is on a specific project page, workflow page, agent detail page, role detail page, or task list, the assistant should use that context to better understand what “this,” “here,” “under this,” “related to this,” or “add one for this” refers to.

The system therefore needs a faster, more natural interface layer that sits on top of the structured data model and current UI context and can interpret user intent accordingly.

Examples of friction this should solve:

* User has to manually open forms and fill fields one by one
* User has to remember where in the system a record type lives
* User has to translate thoughts into the system’s structure manually
* User has to repeat context the app already knows
* Voice-based idea capture and action entry are not smoothly supported
* The assistant may misinterpret intent without awareness of the current page or selected object

⸻

Desired Outcome

The user should be able to open an assistant panel and say or type things like:

* “Create a goal for launching the onboarding prototype.”
* “Make a task called ‘Create web developer role.’”
* “Create a role called Web Developer with instructions saying it is an expert in web development, frontend architecture, responsive UI, and debugging.”
* “Add a task under this project for redesigning the workflow page.”
* “Create an agent based on this role.”
* “Make this into a feature request.”
* “Add a child task for this.”
* “What related tasks already exist here?”

The assistant should:

1. Understand the request
2. Use current UI/page context to interpret references and intent
3. Infer the intended object type when possible
4. Extract structured fields
5. Ask follow-up questions only when required
6. Show the user a preview of what it plans to create or do
7. Let the user confirm, edit, or cancel
8. Create the record(s) in the system

* Basic clarification flow when required

* Deep cross-object reasoning beyond straightforward create/update flows

UX Requirements

Core UX Principles

* Fast
* Low friction
* Clear
* Reversible
* Trustworthy
* Structured without feeling rigid
* Context-aware without feeling opaque

Desired Feel

The assistant should feel like a practical operator layer for the system, not like a generic chatbot. It should be specifically good at understanding the objects, workflows, and current application context of the agent management system.

UX Flow Example

1. User opens assistant
2. User types or pastes transcript
3. Assistant receives current page/context metadata
4. Assistant interprets request using both user input and app context
5. Assistant asks follow-up only if necessary
6. Assistant shows a structured draft
7. User confirms
8. Assistant creates record
9. Assistant returns success message and link to the new record

⸻

Example User Stories

Story 1

As a user, I want to tell the assistant to create a task in plain English so I do not have to manually fill out a task form.

Story 2

As a user, I want the assistant to understand what page or record I am currently viewing so I do not have to restate obvious context.

Story 3

As a user, I want to dictate a role description by voice transcript so I can capture ideas quickly when thinking out loud.

Story 4

As a user, I want the assistant to convert my messy phrasing into structured fields so the system remains organized without slowing me down.

Story 5

As a user, I want to review and confirm what the assistant plans to create before it saves anything.

Story 6

As a user, I want the assistant to understand the system’s objects and my current location well enough that it feels like a specialized operator, not a generic chat box.

⸻

Example Inputs and Expected Behavior

Example 1

Context: User is on project “Onboarding Prototype”
Input:
“Create a task called Create Web Developer Role. Instructions are to create a web developer role that is an expert at web development.”

Expected behavior:
Assistant identifies intent = create task
Extracts:

* name = Create Web Developer Role
* instructions = Create a web developer role that is an expert at web development
* parent project = Onboarding Prototype (from context)

Shows preview, then creates task on confirmation.

⸻

Example 2

Context: User is on role detail page for “Frontend Engineer”
Input:
“Create an agent for this.”

Expected behavior:
Assistant identifies intent = create agent
Infers linked role = Frontend Engineer from context
Shows preview
Creates agent on confirmation

⸻

Example 3

Context: User is on workflow page
Input:
“I need something to track improving this page.”

Expected behavior:
Assistant uses current page context to infer likely relation to workflow or feature work
Still detects ambiguity between task/goal/feature request
Asks:
“Do you want this created as a task, a goal, or a feature request?”

⸻

Technical Implementation Requirements

Architecture Guidance

Build this as a thin assistant layer on top of the existing domain model and create/update services.

Do not hardcode brittle UI-only logic if the same operations already exist in backend/domain services.

Recommended architecture:

1. Assistant UI
2. Context provider / context serializer
3. Assistant orchestration layer
4. Intent parsing / field extraction layer
5. Domain action layer
6. Logging/audit layer

⸻

Suggested System Components

1. Assistant UI Component

Responsible for:

* rendering conversation/messages
* input box
* submission
* preview cards
* clarification prompts
* confirm/edit/cancel actions

2. Context Provider

Responsible for:

* reading current route and UI state
* identifying current object and parent object
* gathering selected/active entities
* exposing context in a clean structured format
* limiting context to useful, relevant information

3. Intent + Extraction Layer

Responsible for:

* interpreting raw natural language
* classifying object type and action type
* using context to improve interpretation
* extracting structured fields
* returning confidence + missing fields

This can initially be rule-assisted and/or LLM-assisted depending on the prototype approach.

4. Action Planner

Responsible for converting interpreted intent plus context into a system action plan like:

{
  "action": "create_task",
  "payload": {
    "name": "Create Web Developer Role",
    "instructions": "Create a web developer role that is an expert at web development.",
    "projectId": "123"
  },
  "contextUsed": {
    "pageType": "project_detail",
    "currentObject": {
      "type": "project",
      "id": "123",
      "name": "Onboarding Prototype"
    }
  },
  "missingFields": [],
  "needsConfirmation": true
}

5. Domain Executor

Responsible for:

* validating payload
* calling actual create services
* returning created object metadata

6. Audit Log

Responsible for:

* storing transcript/input
* context snapshot
* interpreted action
* payload
* outcome

⸻

Recommended V1 Design Strategy

Keep V1 narrow and reliable

Do not try to make it an all-knowing general assistant yet.

Instead, make it very good at:

* understanding a handful of object types
* creating records reliably
* using current app context intelligently
* showing previews
* asking minimal clarifying questions

Prefer a structured intermediate representation

Do not let the assistant directly mutate the database from freeform chat output.

Always convert assistant output into a structured action plan first.

Reuse existing create logic

If tasks, goals, roles, and agents already have forms/services, the assistant should call into those instead of duplicating business logic.

Make context explicit

Do not rely on vague hidden magic. Keep context gathering explicit, inspectable, and debuggable.

Make it debug-friendly

You will want to inspect:

* what the user said
* what context the assistant received
* what the assistant thought they meant
* why it asked a question
* what payload it produced

⸻

Acceptance Criteria

Minimum acceptance criteria for V1

* User can open assistant from the app UI
* User can enter natural language text
* Assistant receives current page/context metadata
* Assistant can interpret requests to create tasks, goals, roles, or agents
* Assistant can use current context to improve interpretation and linking
* Assistant can extract key fields from input
* Assistant asks for clarification when intent is insufficiently clear
* Assistant shows a preview before creation
* User can confirm or cancel
* Record is created successfully in the system after confirmation
* Assistant-generated actions are logged, including context used
* Error states are handled cleanly

⸻

Non-Functional Requirements

Performance

* Assistant interaction should feel responsive
* Preview generation should happen quickly enough to feel conversational

Reliability

* Assistant should fail safely
* Ambiguous cases should not silently create incorrect records

Maintainability

* Assistant action types should be modular and extensible
* New object types and new context providers should be easy to add later

Usability

* The feature should reduce friction, not create more
* The assistant must be simpler than manual form entry for common cases

⸻

Nice-to-Have Features After V1

* Voice recording button with transcription
* Context-aware suggestions based on current page
* “Create from this page”
* “Turn this into a task”
* Edit assistant-created payload before save
* Suggested field autofill
* Multi-record creation from one input
* Search existing records before creating duplicates
* Links to created objects
* In-session contextual memory
* Deeper page-aware assistant actions beyond create flows

⸻

Risks / Challenges

* Overly broad assistant scope could make implementation messy
* Misclassification of user intent could create bad data
* Poor context modeling could cause confusing behavior
* Trying to support too many object types too early could slow progress
* Voice support can become a distraction if included too early
* Trust will drop quickly if the assistant feels generic or inaccurate

⸻

Recommended Phased Implementation

Phase 1

Implement text-only assistant for create flows:

* create task
* create goal
* create role
* create agent

Include:

* current page awareness
* current object awareness
* structured preview and confirmation

Phase 2

Improve disambiguation and context awareness:

* infer parent project/context more reliably
* suggest links to related records
* reduce follow-up questions
* support selected object context

Phase 3

Add voice input:

* record audio
* transcribe
* edit transcript before submit

Phase 4

Support update/search/query flows:

* “show me tasks related to this”
* “update this role”
* “mark this task as blocked”

⸻

Deliverables

The implementation should result in:

1. An assistant UI in the agent management system
2. Natural-language-to-structured-action parsing for V1 object types
3. Context injection from current UI/page state
4. Preview and confirmation flow
5. Record creation integration
6. Action logging
7. Basic documentation for how the assistant works and how to extend it

⸻

Updated Instruction Block for Codex

Implement a V1 in-app context-aware assistant for the agent management system.
Goal:
Create an always-available assistant UI that allows the user to type natural-language instructions and have the system interpret them into structured actions for creating records such as tasks, goals, roles, and agents. The assistant must also use current application context so it can better understand what the user is looking at and interpret instructions more accurately.
Requirements:
- Add an assistant entry point accessible from the main app UI
- Support text input in V1
- Allow users to enter instructions like:
  - “Create a task called Create Web Developer Role”
  - “Create a role named Web Developer that is an expert in frontend development”
  - “Create a goal for improving onboarding”
  - “Create an agent for this”
  - “Add a task under this project”
- Build a context provider that passes structured current-page/app-state context into the assistant, including where possible:
  - current route
  - page type
  - current object type/id/name
  - selected objects
  - parent/breadcrumb context
  - valid actions for the current screen
- Build an interpretation layer that:
  - identifies user intent
  - determines the target object type
  - uses current context to improve interpretation
  - extracts structured fields from freeform input
  - identifies missing required fields
- Before creating anything, show a preview of the interpreted result and require user confirmation
- If intent or required fields are ambiguous, ask a focused clarification question
- After confirmation, create the record using the system’s normal domain/service layer rather than duplicating creation logic
- Log the raw input, context snapshot, interpreted action, extracted payload, and final result for debugging/audit purposes
V1 scope:
- Create flows only
- Supported object types: task, goal, role, agent
- No destructive actions
- No full autonomous multi-step workflows yet
- Voice can be deferred, but structure the code so voice transcript input can be added later
Implementation guidance:
- Keep the assistant architecture modular
- Add a dedicated context provider / serializer layer
- Use a structured intermediate action plan before executing any operation
- Reuse existing validation and creation services
- Optimize for reliability and clarity over cleverness
- Make context usage explicit and debuggable
- Make it easy to extend with more action types later
Deliverables:
- Assistant UI
- Context provider
- Intent/extraction layer
- Preview and confirmation flow
- Record creation integration
- Logging/audit trail
- Clear code organization and brief implementation notes

⸻

Shorter Updated Task Version

Title:
Implement context-aware in-app assistant for agent management system

Description:
Add an always-available assistant to the agent management system that lets the user create structured records such as tasks, goals, roles, and agents using natural language. The assistant should use both the user’s input and the current application context, such as the page being viewed and the current object, to interpret requests more accurately. It should extract relevant fields, ask clarification questions when needed, show a preview before saving, and create records through the normal system logic. Design V1 around text input first, but keep the architecture ready for future voice/transcript input.

Acceptance Criteria:

* Assistant accessible from main UI
* Supports text-based natural language input
* Receives current page/object context
* Can create tasks, goals, roles, and agents
* Uses context to improve interpretation and linking
* Extracts fields from freeform input
* Asks clarifying questions when needed
* Shows preview before create
* Requires confirmation
* Uses existing creation logic
* Logs assistant actions, including context used

delegationPacket.inputContext:
Feature Name

In-App Context-Aware Assistant for Structured System Actions

Summary

Implement an always-available assistant inside the agent management system that allows the user to interact with the system using natural language and, eventually, voice input. The assistant should understand requests like creating goals, tasks, roles, agents, workflows, or other records, ask for clarification when needed, and convert user intent into structured actions within the system.

The assistant should also be context-aware, meaning it should have access to relevant information about what the user is currently viewing or interacting with in the application. This should help it interpret instructions more accurately, infer relationships more intelligently, reduce unnecessary clarification questions, and perform more relevant actions.

The purpose of this feature is to reduce friction when using the agent management system by allowing the user to simply tell the system what they want created or changed instead of manually navigating forms and screens for every action.

⸻

Problem

The current system likely requires too much manual interaction to create or manage structured records such as tasks, goals, roles, and instructions. This adds friction, especially when the user already knows what they want and could describe it much faster through speech or text.

A generic assistant without awareness of the current application state would still force the user to over-explain things that the system should already know. If the user is on a specific project page, workflow page, agent detail page, role detail page, or task list, the assistant should use that context to better understand what “this,” “here,” “under this,” “related to this,” or “add one for this” refers to.

The system therefore needs a faster, more natural interface layer that sits on top of the structured data model and current UI context and can interpret user intent accordingly.

Examples of friction this should solve:

* User has to manually open forms and fill fields one by one
* User has to remember where in the system a record type lives
* User has to translate thoughts into the system’s structure manually
* User has to repeat context the app already knows
* Voice-based idea capture and action entry are not smoothly supported
* The assistant may misinterpret intent without awareness of the current page or selected object

⸻

Desired Outcome

The user should be able to open an assistant panel and say or type things like:

* “Create a goal for launching the onboarding prototype.”
* “Make a task called ‘Create web developer role.’”
* “Create a role called Web Developer with instructions saying it is an expert in web development, frontend architecture, responsive UI, and debugging.”
* “Add a task under this project for redesigning the workflow page.”
* “Create an agent based on this role.”
* “Make this into a feature request.”
* “Add a child task for this.”
* “What related tasks already exist here?”

The assistant should:

1. Understand the request
2. Use current UI/page context to interpret references and intent
3. Infer the intended object type when possible
4. Extract structured fields
5. Ask follow-up questions only when required
6. Show the user a preview of what it plans to create or do
7. Let the user confirm, edit, or cancel
8. Create the record(s) in the system

⸻

Primary Goal

Create a usable, context-aware assistant interface that can translate conversational input into structured system actions for the agent management system.

⸻

Scope for V1

V1 should focus on a narrow but useful version of the feature.

V1 should support:

* Text input to the assistant
* Optional paste-in transcript input
* Interpreting requests to create:
    * Tasks
    * Goals
    * Roles
    * Agents
* Structured field extraction from natural language
* Awareness of current page, selected object, and relevant local context
* Preview before save
* Confirmation before record creation
* Basic clarification flow when required
* Error handling when intent is unclear
* Logging of assistant actions, context used, and created records

V1 should not try to fully solve:

* Complex multi-step workflow orchestration
* Fully autonomous execution of broad system changes
* Advanced voice/audio recording pipeline if that adds too much complexity
* Deep cross-object reasoning beyond straightforward create/update flows
* Open-ended destructive actions without confirmation

⸻

Context Awareness Requirement

Definition

The assistant should be aware of relevant application context at the time the user submits a request. This means it should receive structured information about what the user is currently viewing or interacting with so that it can interpret the request more accurately.

This context may include:

* current page or route
* current object type
* current object ID
* currently viewed record details
* selected item(s)
* parent object or breadcrumb context
* surrounding list/filter/search state if relevant
* current project/workflow/role/agent/task the user is inside
* available actions valid for the current context

Examples

If the user is on:

* a project page, “create a task for this” should default to creating a task linked to that project
* a role detail page, “create an agent for this” should infer that “this” refers to the current role
* a workflow page, “add a step about validation” should infer the workflow as parent context
* a task detail page, “make this blocked” or “add a child task” should use that task as the target
* an agent list page, “create one for documentation” should infer that the user likely means an agent, not some unrelated object type

The assistant should reduce ambiguity by using available context, but still show a preview before committing actions.

⸻

Functional Requirements

1. Assistant Entry Point

Implement an assistant that is easy to access from anywhere in the agent management system.

Possible UX patterns:

* Persistent floating assistant button
* Slide-over panel
* Global command bar with assistant mode
* Dedicated assistant page with chat history

Minimum requirement:

* Assistant can be opened from the main application UI without deep navigation

⸻

2. Input Modes

V1

* Text input
* Support for pasted voice transcripts

V2+

* Direct voice recording
* Speech-to-text transcription
* Optional playback and transcript editing before submit

⸻

3. Context Injection

When the assistant is opened or when the user submits a request, the system should provide the assistant with structured context about the current UI state.

This context should be passed in a controlled, structured form rather than forcing the assistant to infer everything from raw rendered text.

Example context payload:

{
  "route": "/projects/123/tasks",
  "pageType": "project_detail",
  "currentObject": {
    "type": "project",
    "id": "123",
    "name": "Onboarding Prototype"
  },
  "selectedObjects": [],
  "breadcrumbs": [
    { "type": "workspace", "id": "w1", "name": "AdventureWare" },
    { "type": "project", "id": "123", "name": "Onboarding Prototype" }
  ],
  "visibleCapabilities": [
    "create_task",
    "create_goal",
    "view_project",
    "link_existing_task"
  ]
}

The assistant should use this context to improve intent recognition and payload generation.

⸻

4. Intent Recognition

The assistant should classify what the user is trying to do.

Initial supported intents:

* Create task
* Create goal
* Create role
* Create agent
* Ask system question
* Request clarification/help

Intent recognition should consider:

* the user’s raw input
* the current page context
* the currently viewed object
* the kinds of actions that are valid in the current location

The system should infer likely intent from phrasing and context, but not rely on exact keywords.

⸻

5. Structured Field Extraction

The assistant should extract relevant fields from the user’s natural-language input.

Example mappings

Task

* name/title
* description/instructions
* parent object/project if mentioned or inferred from context
* priority if mentioned
* tags if useful
* status default

Goal

* name/title
* description
* timeframe if mentioned
* parent area/project if mentioned or inferred from context

Role

* name/title
* description
* instructions
* specialty/focus areas

Agent

* name/title
* linked role if mentioned or inferred from context
* instructions
* capabilities or tools if supported
* purpose

The assistant should populate as much as possible automatically, including context-derived defaults, then present a draft.

⸻

6. Clarification Flow

If the user gives incomplete or ambiguous instructions, the assistant should ask focused follow-up questions.

Examples:

* “Do you want this created as a task or a goal?”
* “Should this role also include backend development, or just frontend/web?”
* “Should I attach this to the current project?”
* “Do you want me to create the role only, or also create an agent that uses it?”

Clarification should be minimal and only used when required after considering available context.

⸻

7. Preview Before Commit

Before saving, the assistant should show a structured preview of what it plans to create.

Example:

Create Role

* Name: Web Developer
* Description: Expert in web development
* Instructions: Specializes in frontend architecture, responsive UI, debugging, and web app implementation
* Context Used: Current page = Roles, Parent workspace = AdventureWare

Actions:

* Confirm
* Edit
* Cancel

This is important because the assistant may interpret things imperfectly and should not silently create bad records.

⸻

8. Record Creation

After confirmation, the assistant should create the record in the system using the same underlying creation logic used by standard forms or APIs.

Important:

* Assistant-created records should use the same validation rules as manually created records
* No special inconsistent backend path
* Reuse domain services where possible
* Context-derived relationships should still be validated before commit

⸻

9. Action Log / Traceability

Every assistant action should be logged.

Track:

* raw user input
* interpreted intent
* extracted fields
* context snapshot used for interpretation
* follow-up questions asked
* final confirmed payload
* result of creation
* errors if any

This will help debugging, trust, refinement, and prompt iteration.

⸻

10. Safety / Guardrails

The assistant should not perform destructive or high-risk actions without clear confirmation.

For V1:

* Focus primarily on create flows
* If update/delete is added later, always require explicit confirmation
* If the assistant is uncertain, it should say so instead of guessing aggressively
* Context should improve confidence, not justify unsafe silent actions

⸻

UX Requirements

Core UX Principles

* Fast
* Low friction
* Clear
* Reversible
* Trustworthy
* Structured without feeling rigid
* Context-aware without feeling opaque

Desired Feel

The assistant should feel like a practical operator layer for the system, not like a generic chatbot. It should be specifically good at understanding the objects, workflows, and current application context of the agent management system.

UX Flow Example

1. User opens assistant
2. User types or pastes transcript
3. Assistant receives current page/context metadata
4. Assistant interprets request using both user input and app context
5. Assistant asks follow-up only if necessary
6. Assistant shows a structured draft
7. User confirms
8. Assistant creates record
9. Assistant returns success message and link to the new record

Story 2

Story 3

Story 4

Story 5

Story 6

Shows preview, then creates task on confirmation.

⸻

Infers linked role = Frontend Engineer from context
Shows preview

Add voice input:

* record audio
* transcribe
* edit transcript before submit

Phase 4

Support update/search/query flows:

* “show me tasks related to this”
* “update this role”
* “mark this task as blocked”

⸻

Deliverables

The implementation should result in:

1. An assistant UI in the agent management system
2. Natural-language-to-structured-action parsing for V1 object types
3. Context injection from current UI/page state
4. Preview and confirmation flow
5. Record creation integration
6. Action logging
7. Basic documentation for how the assistant works and how to extend it

⸻

Updated Instruction Block for Codex

Implement a V1 in-app context-aware assistant for the agent management system.
Goal:
Create an always-available assistant UI that allows the user to type natural-language instructions and have the system interpret them into structured actions for creating records such as tasks, goals, roles, and agents. The assistant must also use current application context so it can better understand what the user is looking at and interpret instructions more accurately.
Requirements:
- Add an assistant entry point accessible from the main app UI
- Support text input in V1
- Allow users to enter instructions like:
  - “Create a task called Create Web Developer Role”
  - “Create a role named Web Developer that is an expert in frontend development”
  - “Create a goal for improving onboarding”
  - “Create an agent for this”
  - “Add a task under this project”
- Build a context provider that passes structured current-page/app-state context into the assistant, including where possible:
  - current route
  - page type
  - current object type/id/name
  - selected objects
  - parent/breadcrumb context
  - valid actions for the current screen
- Build an interpretation layer that:
  - identifies user intent
  - determines the target object type
  - uses current context to improve interpretation
  - extracts structured fields from freeform input
  - identifies missing required fields
- Before creating anything, show a preview of the interpreted result and require user confirmation
- If intent or required fields are ambiguous, ask a focused clarification question
- After confirmation, create the record using the system’s normal domain/service layer rather than duplicating creation logic
- Log the raw input, context snapshot, interpreted action, extracted payload, and final result for debugging/audit purposes
V1 scope:
- Create flows only
- Supported object types: task, goal, role, agent
- No destructive actions
- No full autonomous multi-step workflows yet
- Voice can be deferred, but structure the code so voice transcript input can be added later
Implementation guidance:
- Keep the assistant architecture modular
- Add a dedicated context provider / serializer layer
- Use a structured intermediate action plan before executing any operation
- Reuse existing validation and creation services
- Optimize for reliability and clarity over cleverness
- Make context usage explicit and debuggable
- Make it easy to extend with more action types later
Deliverables:
- Assistant UI
- Context provider
- Intent/extraction layer
- Preview and confirmation flow
- Record creation integration
- Logging/audit trail
- Clear code organization and brief implementation notes

⸻

Shorter Updated Task Version

Title:
Implement context-aware in-app assistant for agent management system

Description:
Add an always-available assistant to the agent management system that lets the user create structured records such as tasks, goals, roles, and agents using natural language. The assistant should use both the user’s input and the current application context, such as the page being viewed and the current object, to interpret requests more accurately. It should extract relevant fields, ask clarification questions when needed, show a preview before saving, and create records through the normal system logic. Design V1 around text input first, but keep the architecture ready for future voice/transcript input.

Acceptance Criteria:

* Assistant accessible from main UI
* Supports text-based natural language input
* Receives current page/object context
* Can create tasks, goals, roles, and agents
